name: Runtime Sanitizers
def: Compiler-instrumented runtime checks that detect memory, threading and UB errors during execution.
problem: Catch memory-safety and data-race bugs that crash rarely or corrupt silently.
mechanism: Tools like ASan, TSan, UBSan, MSan instrument the binary to detect out-of-bounds, use-after-free, races and undefined behaviour at the moment they occur.
benefits: Pinpoints memory/race bugs at the fault
Far better than random crashes later
whenNot: Memory-safe languages (less need); production hot paths
myths: "Memory-safe languages need sanitizers too" — Rust/Java rarely need ASan; C/C++ critically do
